summary: The July 1 selection emphasizes finite-density QGP chemical equilibration and hard photons, baryon-antibaryon symmetry energy in low-energy relativistic heavy-ion matter, hyperon spin correlations as an open-quantum-system view of hadronization, and HEP-facing AI/ML for profiled unbinned likelihoods, detector-aware particle-flow reconstruction, and surrogate-accelerated Bayesian inference.
strongest_paper_summary: The strongest heavy-ion paper is arXiv:2606.31749, which connects finite-density chemical non-equilibrium to hard thermal photon production in a controlled QGP expansion model. The strongest AI/ML items are arXiv:2602.13184 for nuisance-aware unbinned likelihood inference and arXiv:2606.30485 for robust, detector-aware reconstruction targets.

papers.title: Finite-Density Dynamics of Chemically Equilibrating QGP in Conformal Gubser Flow and Hard Thermal Photon Production
papers.abstract: Studies chemical equilibration of finite-baryon-density QGP in conformal Gubser flow using fugacity evolution coupled to hydrodynamic expansion, finding delayed equilibration, suppressed total thermal photon yield, and enhanced early high-pT photon production.

papers.title: Symmetry energy of baryon- and neutron-rich nuclear matter
papers.authors_short: Zhi-Ying Qin; Jia Zhou; Jun Xu
papers.abstract: Relativistic mean-field study, assuming G-parity invariance, of baryon- and neutron-rich matter in low-energy relativistic heavy-ion collisions, defining a baryon-antibaryon symmetry energy and analyzing nucleon/antinucleon potential splittings.

papers.title: Lambda anti-Lambda spin correlations in high-energy collisions from quantum channels: an open quantum system view of hadronization
papers.authors_short: Joao Barata; Ivan Cuntin; Enrique Rico; Bin Wu
papers.abstract: Models hyperon spin-pair correlations through quantum channels, arguing that data are consistent with a two-qubit depolarizing channel and deriving a Lindblad open-system interpretation of hadronization spin dynamics.

papers.title: Profiling systematic uncertainties in Simulation-Based Inference with Factorizable Normalizing Flows
papers.authors_short: Davide Valsecchi; Mauro Donega; Rainer Wallny
papers.abstract: Uses factorized normalizing flows to model systematic variations as parametric density deformations, with amortized nuisance dependence and Poisson-bootstrap neural uncertainty for profiled unbinned HEP likelihoods.

papers.title: Detector-aware target definitions for full-event particle reconstruction
papers.authors_short: Katharina Schaeuble; Alessandro Brusamolino; Dolores Garcia; Jan Kieseler
papers.abstract: Introduces detector-aware merged targets for hit-level particle-flow reconstruction, showing improved performance and robustness under changes in event topology with a fixed GNN reconstruction model.

papers.title: Multipolar Magnetic-Field Inference for PSR J0740+6620 with Neural-Network-Accelerated NICER Pulse-Profile Modeling
papers.authors_short: Farhana Taiyebah; Constantinos Kalapotharakos; Greg Olmschenk; Wendy F. Wallace; and 5 others
papers.abstract: Trains CNN surrogates on 51.2 million synthetic pulse profiles to accelerate 11-dimensional NICER pulse-profile MCMC inference by more than a factor of 400, while retaining broad multimodal posteriors.

papers.title: Comparative study of ensemble-based uncertainty quantification methods for neural network interatomic potentials
papers.authors_short: Yonatan Kurniawan; Mingjian Wen; Ellad B. Tadmor; Mark K. Transtrum
papers.abstract: Systematic evaluation of ensemble UQ for neural-network interatomic potentials, showing that predictive precision can fail as a proxy for accuracy in out-of-distribution system-level predictions.
